comment: Authoring catalog (thin). Every entry has a `kind`. `web` apps ship as a SHA-pinned GitHub Release zip of the built app and run in-shell. `native` apps declare an install source: `installers` (package-manager ids: brew/winget/scoop/flatpak), `downloads` (per-platform direct URLs), and/or `source` (a GitHub repo + per-platform asset-name regex). A resolver (scripts/resolve-catalog.mjs, run on a schedule) expands `source` entries via the GitHub API into registry/resolved.json — version, download URLs, release notes, screenshots, metrics — which the shell fetches at runtime. `replaces` maps the app to the paid product(s) it is an alternative to. Contributors publish by PR-ing an entry here.

apps.name: Presenter
apps.description: AI presentation builder — chat edits the real code of each deck, with live preview, present mode, notes and timer.
apps.author: Albert Marimón

apps.name: GIMP
apps.description: Full-featured raster image editor — layers, masks, filters, scripting.
apps.author: The GIMP Team

apps.name: LocalSend
apps.description: Share files and messages to nearby devices over the local network — no internet, no accounts.
apps.author: LocalSend

apps.name: draw.io
apps.description: Diagramming app — flowcharts, UML, network and architecture diagrams, fully offline.
apps.author: JGraph

apps.name: Logseq
apps.description: Local-first outliner and knowledge base over plain markdown with backlinks and graph view.
apps.author: Logseq
